
### an OOO pipeline {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipeline.figure.svg){width=900px}

### an OOO pipeline (branch prediction) {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineBP.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow}

branch prediction needs to happen before instructions decoded <br />
done with cache-like tables of information about recent branches

:::

### an OOO pipeline (register renaming) {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineRename.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-out fragment-index=1}

register renaming after decoding <br />
where mapping from architectural to physical names kept

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-in fragment-index=1}

"dispatch" instructions = add to instruction queue <br/>
also requires preparing reorder buffer (for handling squashing)

:::

### an OOO pipeline (instruction queue) {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineIQ.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow}

instruction queue holds pending renamed instructions <br />
combined with register-ready info to <i>issue</i> instructions <br/>
(<i>issue</i> = start executing)

:::

### an OOO pipeline (starting instruction) {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineRegread.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-out fragment-index=1}

after selecting from instruction queue, <br/>
read from large register file and handle forwarding

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-in fragment-index=1}

typically read 6+ registers at a time <br/>
extra data paths for forwarding (not drawn)

:::

### an OOO pipeline (execution units) {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineExecute.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow}

many <i>execution units</i> actually do math or cache load/store<br />
some may have multiple pipeline stages<br />
some may take variable time (data cache, integer divide, ...)

:::


### an OOO pipeline (writeback) {.nostretch}


![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineWB.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow}

writeback to physical registers <br />
register file typically supports writing 3+ registers at a time

:::

### an OOO pipeline (commit) {.nostretch}

![](/ooo/texfig/oooPipelineCommit.figure.svg){width=900px}

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-out fragment-index=1}

new commit (sometimes <i>retire</i>) stage finalized instruction <br/>
figures out when physical registers can be reused

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-in fragment-index=1}

also tracks bookkeeping information if we need to undo instruction <br />
(because of branch misprediction/segfault/etc.) <br />

:::
